what_it_does: Records a channel attachment entry that links the design-assets channel to a specific image asset target.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
standouts: Target URI uses an image:// scheme pointing to icons/Feature_3_Floorplan
It is typed as an images attachment in the design-assets channel
conventions: Channel-memory attachment stored as a standalone markdown file
concerns: Target URI is opaque: no file extension, dimensions, format, or checksum provided
No alt text, title, or descriptive caption
Only an added timestamp; no modified/removed timestamp
notes: A minimal design-assets channel image attachment stub that points to the Feature_3_Floorplan icon asset.
questions: Is image://design-assets/icons/Feature_3_Floorplan resolvable locally, remotely, or as a virtual path?
Who added this attachment and is it versioned?
What are the image format, dimensions, and file size?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is an attachment record referencing an image target.
intention_signals: To catalog an image attachment in the design-assets channel so the Feature_3_Floorplan icon can be discovered and referenced by a stable target URI.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel and a named image asset, giving the system a discoverable attachment record.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record (att-dcafbcd3) in the design-assets channel that references the image target image://design-assets/icons/Feature_3_Floorplan and records its addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to catalog an image attachment so the design-assets channel can reference the Feature_3_Floorplan icon.
